Wu's Fighting Sash  
 


Slot: WAIST
AC: 4
AGI: +2
WT: 0.1 Size: SMALL
Class: MNK BST
Race: HUM BAR ELF TRL OGR IKS VAH FRG DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Wu's Fighting Sash

Slot: WAIST
AC: 4
AGI: +2
WT: 0.1 Size: SMALL
Class: MNK BST
Race: HUM BAR ELF TRL OGR IKS VAH FRG DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Armor
Stackable:No

  • Wu's Fighting Sash (Tailoring, 128 trivial)

    Expansion: Original

Crafted by the tailoring skill, and an Enchanter


Uploaded November 27th, 2008
Send a correction
Post Comment
Correction: Trivial is 128 Tailoring
QuoteReply
# Nov 07 2005 at 11:25 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1606090783103
242 posts
Wu's Fighting Sash trivials at 128 rather than 135.

BV
▲ Page top
Tailoring.
QuoteReply
# Aug 08 2001 at 11:58 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361106273
5 posts
Actually, Wu's Fighting Armor trivials around 158 in tailoring. The Wu's Fighting Gauntlets (which were the first piece of tailored Wu Armor created ) do trivial around 88, but the remaining pieces range from 131 to 158.

The easiest way to get Wu's Armor is to go to East Karanas and try to get as much spider silk as you can carry. Any tailor worth his salt would be thrilled to trade you armor for raw materials as the Wu's Armor is the only way to get to 158 currently. For further information on the trivial levels on this stuff, check out www.eqtraders.com.
▲ Page top
making Wu's clothing
QuoteReply
# Jul 04 2001 at 9:39 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Wu's clothing has a trivial lvl of 88 in tailoring. But is NOT cheap to make. You have to get an Enchanter to make the Viscous Mana to make this armor. The cheapest I have bought the mana was 25pp a pop so you want to make sure your tailoring skill is up there because you'll cry having failures. to make the Wu's fighting guantlets you need..1 glove pattern...1 silk swatch...1 greater lightstone...1 vial of viscous mana...and 4 heady kiolas.{ Master brewer made..trivial at 135}..guants are AC4..weight 0.1 and are a light source..Good lucky
▲ Page top
useful
QuoteReply
# Jun 10 2001 at 8:29 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Alright some of you people see crappy stats and say this sucks cuz the stats look crappy
im a level 59 monk on Mithaniel i retired a while back and gave all my gear away back then and now im regaining im getting my golden tranq sash next week on a raid now i dont know about your wack opinions but im wearing a set of this and i have to say it kicks a@@ all that agility at high level = bookoo AC at least 120 ac or more so for a iksar this is crazy ac add sure its not the best stuff out there but in terms of a good cheap alternative for non twink people who play the game an earn their gear with their respective chars this stuff is pretty good remember a monk needs his ac and more agi more ac enough saif
▲ Page top
Don't sell this thing short.
QuoteReply
# Jun 02 2001 at 7:09 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Hi all, I was just wanting to throw in my own thoughts, a fellow offered me a full suit of this, I only wanted a few pieces but he made me a good deal on it. So I was looking at this sash for a bit and getting ready to hand it to a newbie or bank it when I thought, hey, why not wear it for a bit? My Thick Leather Apron was getting tired anyway. Know what? The Ac hit isn't all that bad. And since I use weapons and haven't raised the cash for a weight reduction bag better than Evil Eye, weight is an issure for me. Going from a 1wt belt to a 0.1wt belt dropped me down to 16wt, which is the line where I start losing Ac at level 32. So my total Ac loss was only about 1 or so. And I can't complain about the 2agi that I got from the bargain. Anyway, just wanted to let you know that the Thick leather apron is not the vast improvement over this that everybody says it is. And one question: What skill is needed to tailor this stuff?

Manji Sevenfists
32nd Fist, disciple of the Tranquil
Tunare Server

why wasting the time getting faction all the quest stuff is droppable :) so just make a mule and use him to turn the items in or get a friend to do it for you.

Then I would work on my faction. You will need to complete all your sash quests(FP), and all your headband quests(Qeynos) if you plan on having your epic.

freeport sashes

this seems useless when the yellow sash is clearly better stat wise and all you need are things found in EC. i hope no monks buy this without first going to freeport and earning a much better one
▲ Page top
Durr, I'm level three.
QuoteReply
# May 25 2001 at 2:17 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361345059
4 posts
How the hell is a yellow sash:
Sta: +1 Agi: +1 Cha: +1 HP: +1

Better than this:
AC: +4 Agi: +2

The 4 AC alone makes it better than pretty much any of the quest sashes out there.

However, if a monk wants a cheap waist slot item, just get a Thick Leather Apron (8 AC, 1 weight). Get this. It's better than any of the quest sashes aside from the weight. WOW.

RE: freeport sashes
QuoteReply
# May 25 2001 at 11:54 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592757113487
Scholar
119 posts
kill orcs in EC/WC. work on your faction when young and can get exp. I've only been playing since december, but i read up on the boards for myself and got my freeport faction up due to my Epic is in there.

word of advice, kill orcs, don't turn in belts. (i turned in 85 of them to the mitila captain). the knights still don't like me, but i earned half of level 17 from them...

▲ Page top
RE: freeport sashes
QuoteReply
# May 27 2001 at 8:29 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Ok first off u need to turn in the belts first (which i suggest u get the help of a druid with wolf form or if u have sneak someone who can invis ) turn them into the Militia house Captain something-or-other, to get militia faction. Once u are Dubious with them, THEN work on knight faction by killing tons of orcs, ooh fun weeee 8)! An alternative to turning in belts for militia faction, the orc/gnoll scalp quest in Highhold Pass also gives Freeport Militia faction without other faction hits ). Ta Dah! there u go u now have access to almost all of FP =).


Lone Iksar Monk of 33 winters
